Definition: Internal UUID identifying the report a group of records is associated with. This may be used to join to other datasets captured within the same report. Corresponds to guid_result in Speedtest F/MNPD Extracts.
Internal UUID uniquely identifying this record.
Denotes the platform on which the test was taken:1 = Android2 = iOS

Indicator for which activity triggered the scan: timer: scan triggered by timer (approximately every 15 minutes)motion: scan triggered by motion from device sensors (deprecated)location update intent: scan triggered by another application requesting a locationlocation update callback: scan triggered by another application receiving a location
Date and time when scan was taken according to the device's time and date.
Date and time that Ookla received the measurement result from the device.

Each individual device is assigned a unique ID, which is tied to all the tests taken on the device. A durable identifier for the Speedtest data collection platform; non-durable for the QoE data collection platform.
Name of the local time zone where the scan was taken

Android Device API returned manufacturer of the product/hardware.
Android Device API returned consumer-visible brand with which the product/hardware will be associated, if any.
Common name (or marketed name) of the device; based on Ookla-maintained lookup tables
Common name of the device manufacturer
Version of the operating system used at the time of the scan. Reports the API level (example: 30). See https://source.android.com/setup/start/build-numbers for reference.
The version string for the radio firmware.
TRUE if the device is a world phone.
The number of SIMs registered to the device during the test.

The number of logical modems currently configured to be activated. 0 = voice, sms, data is not supported 1 = single standby mode (single SIM functionality) 2 = dual standby mode (dual SIM functionality)3 = tri standby mode (tri SIM functionality)
The number of logical modem that can be potentially active simultaneously, in terms of hardware capability.
TRUE if the current device is "data capable" over a radio on the device. "Data capable" means that this device supports packet-switched data connections over the telephony network.
TRUE if mobile data is enabled or not per user setting.

TRUE if a cellular data connection is allowed in the device.Whether cellular data connection is allowed considers all factors below:User turned on data setting isDataEnabled().Carrier allows data to be on.Network policy.And possibly others.
TRUE if mobile data roaming is enabled on the subscription.
TRUE if ICC card present in the device. An ICC card is a smart card that contains a subscriber identity module (SIM) and is used to identify and authenticate users to a mobile network.
Text field describing the service state (in service, emergency only, power off, etc)
A flag indicating if voice over PS (e.g. VoLTE) is available in the cell.
TRUE if the phone is registered to a mobile network that provides service on this cell and this cell is being used or would be used for network signaling.

The state of the display.0 = STATE_UNKNOWN, the display state is unknown1 = STATE_OFF, the display is off2 = STATE_ON, the display is on3 = STATE_DOZE, the display is dozing in a low power state; it is still on but is optimized for showing system-provided content while the device is non-interactive4 = STATE_DOZE_SUSPEND, the display is dozing in a suspended low power state; it is still on but the CPU is not updating it. This may be used in one of two ways: to show static system-provided content while the device is non-interactive, or to allow a "Sidekick" compute resource to update the display. For this reason, the CPU must not control the display in this mode.6 = STATE_ON_SUSPEND, the display is in a suspended full power state; it is still on but the CPU is not updating it. This may be used in one of two ways: to show static system-provided content while the device is non-interactive, or to allow a "Sidekick" compute resource to update the display. For this reason, the CPU must not control the display in this mode.

The current thermal status of the device. -1 = thermal status error0 = not under thermal throttling1 = light throttling where UX is not impacted2 = moderate throttling where UX is not largely impacted3 = severe throttling where UX is largely impacted4 = critical, platform has done everything to reduce power5 = emergency, key components in platform are shutting down due to thermal condition. Device functionalities will be limited.6 = shutdown needed immediately

An estimate of how much thermal headroom the device currently has before hitting severe throttling. Values range from 0.0 to 1.0, where 1.0 indicates severe throttling threshold. Values may exceed 1.0, but there is no implied mapping to specific thermal status levels beyond that point.

True when the cellular data access network technology is NR. Useful for indicating if the underlying cellular connection is NR while connected to WiFi as the active data bearer for 5G standalone implementations.

New Radio state of the mobile data network. Returns one of the following: 1 = RESTRICTED. The device is camped on an LTE cell that supports E-UTRA-NR Dual Connectivity(EN-DC) but either the use of dual connectivity with NR(DCNR) is restricted or NR is not supported by the selected PLMN.2 = NOT_RESTRICTED. The device is camped on an LTE cell that supports E-UTRA-NR Dual Connectivity(EN-DC) and both the use of dual connectivity with NR(DCNR) is not restricted and NR is supported by the selected PLMN.3 = CONNECTED. The device is camped on an LTE cell that supports E-UTRA-NR Dual Connectivity(EN-DC) and also connected to at least one 5G cell as a secondary serving cell.0 = NONE. The device isn't camped on an LTE cell or the LTE cell doesn't support E-UTRA-NR Dual Connectivity(EN-DC).

Indicates if a network type override occurred.0 = no override1 = LTE with carrier aggregation2 = LTE advanced pro3 = NR NSA4 = NR NSA mmWave5 = NR advanced (NR network with data rate that is higher than the generic 5G rate such as mmWave, unique bands, or carrier aggregation)
Estimated first hop downstream bandwidth for the network (in Kbps).
Upstream bandwidth for the network (in Kbps). This only refers to the estimated first hop transport bandwidth.

Indicates the type of activity on a data connection (cellular).0 = DATA_ACTIVITY_NONE, no traffic1 = DATA_ACTIVITY_IN, currently receiving IP PPP traffic2 = DATA_ACTIVITY_OUT, currently sending IP PPP traffic3 = DATA_ACTIVITY_INOUT, currently both sending and receiving IP PPP traffic4 = DATA_ACTIVITY_DORMANT, data connection is active, but physical link is down

Indicates the current data connection state (cellular).0 = DATA_DISCONNECTED, IP traffic not available1 = DATA_CONNECTING, currently setting up a data connection2 = DATA_CONNECTED, IP traffic should be available3 = DATA_SUSPENDED, the connection is up, but IP traffic is temporarily unavailable. For example, in a 2G network, data activity may be suspended when a voice call arrives.4 = DATA_DISCONNECTING, IP traffic may be available but will cease working imminently5 = DATA_HANDOVER_IN_PROGRESS

True if any of the following fields were sourced from Android's TelephonyManager rather than CellInfoNr: ss_rsrp, ss_rsrq, ss_sinr, csi_rsrp, csi_rsrq, csi_sinr, nr_level, nr_arfcn, nci, nr_pci, nr_tac, nr_mcc, nr_mnc Otherwise, "False".
Reference signal received power; this field is pre-filtered to between -144 and -44

CSI Reference Signal Received Power (CSI-RSRP), is defined as the linear average over the power contributions (in [W]) of the resource elements of the antenna port(s) that carry CSI reference signals configured for RSRP measurements within the considered measurement frequency bandwidth in the configured CSI-RS occasions. Reference: 3GPP TS 38.215.

Synchronization Signal Reference Signal Received Power (SS-RSRP) is defined as the linear average over the power contributions (in [W]) of the resource elements that carry secondary synchronization signals. Reference: 3GPP TS 38.215.
Reference signal received quality; this field is pre-filtered to between -20 and -3

CSI Reference Signal Received Quality (CSI-RSRQ) is defined as the ratio of N*CSI-RSRP to CSI-RSSI, where N is the number of resource blocks in the CSI-RSSI measurement bandwidth. Reference: 3GPP TS 38.215.

Synchronization Signal Reference Signal Received Quality (SS-RSRQ) is defined as the ratio of N*SS-RSRP / NR carrier RSSI, where N is the number of resource blocks in the NR carrier RSSI measurement bandwidth. Reference: 3GPP TS 38.215.

LTE RSSNR (Reference Signal Signal-to-Noise Ratio) is a measure (in units of dB) of the LTE signal quality, calculated as the ratio between the average signal level of Reference Signals (RS) over the wideband noise power.

CSI signal-to-noise and interference ratio (CSI-SINR), is defined as the linear average over the power contribution (in [W]) of the resource elements carrying CSI reference signals divided by the linear average of the noise and interference power contribution (in [W]). Reference: 3GPP TS 38.215.

SS signal-to-noise and interference ratio (SS-SINR), is defined as the linear average over the power contribution (in [W]) of the resource elements carrying secondary synchronisation signals divided by the linear average of the noise and interference power contribution (in [W]). Reference: 3GPP TS 38.215.
Received signal strength indicator; this field is pre-filtered to between -113 and -51
LTE Timing Advance measured in Ts. 2147483647 represents unknown.
Channel Quality Indicator. In LTE, there are 15 different CQI values ranging from 1 to 15 and mapping between CQI and modulation scheme.
Energy per chip over the noise spectral density (Ec/No) as dB. The Ec/No of the measured cell in the range [-24, 1] or UNAVAILABLE.
Received Signal Strength Indicator in Arbitrary Strength Unit (ASU) units. Asu is calculated based on 3GPP RSRP. Refer to 3GPP 27.007 (Ver 10.3.0) Sec 8.69.
Region name where test is taken. This is the third-order civil entity in a country level. Depending on the country, these are the level under counties. Not all countries display this information.

The frequency range of 5G NR. 0 = FREQUENCY_RANGE_UNKNOWN. Indicates frequency range is unknown.1 = FREQUENCY_RANGE_LOW. Indicates the frequency range is below 1GHz.2 = FREQUENCY_RANGE_MID. Indicates the frequency range is between 1GHz to 3GHz.3 = FREQUENCY_RANGE_HIGH. Indicates the frequency range is between 3GHz and 6GHz.4 = FREQUENCY_RANGE_MMWAVE. Indicates the frequency range is above 6GHz.

LTE Physical Cell Identity. An integer to identify the physical LTE cell the user is connected to. The value is unique to the physical cell antennae rather than a specific cell tower. Valid values are 0 to 503. A value of 65535 or null indicates that the device was unable to return a PCI value.
The standard name of the cellular technology associated with the cell (GSM, CDMA, WCDMA, LTE, NR)
The detailed name of the cellular technology associated with the cell (e.g., NrNSA, NrSA, LTECA, HSPA+). Available for the Speedtest data collection platform.
The mobile network generation (2G, 3G, 4G, or 5G) of the cellular connection (derived from the connected cell info).
LTE Tracking Area Code. A 16 bit integer used to facilitate handoff of a device between cells. The Tracking Area Identity can be determined by prepending the MCC and MNC to the Tracking Area Code.
GSM, WCDMA. A 16-bit integer representing a cell's location within a given operator's system. 65535 represents unknown.
WCDMA (or UMTS). Primary Scrambling Code. Valid values range from 0 to 512 with 65535 representing unknown.
A flag indicating if dual-connectivity with NR is restricted in the cell.
A flag indicating if EUTRAN NR dual-connectivity is available in the cell.

The Absolute Radio Frequency Channel Number (ARFCN) is a unique number given to each radio channel in GSM. The ARFCN can be used to calculate the exact frequency of the radio channel. Within the GSM900 band ARFCN 1 to 124 are used. In the GSM1800 band ARFCN 512 to 885 are used. The UTRA Absolute Radio Frequency Channel Number (UARFCN) is a unique number given to each radio channel within the frequency bands used by the UMTS UTRA. The UARFCN can be used to calculate the carrier frequency. The E-UTRA Absolute Radio Frequency Channel Number (EARFCN). In LTE, the carrier frequency in the uplink and downlink is designated by EARFCN, which ranges between 0-65535. EARFCN uniquely identify the LTE band and carrier frequency. EARFCN is independent of channel bandwidth.
The type of cell frequency channel. Values: NRARFCN, ARFCN, UARFCN, EARFCN.
New Radio Absolute Radio Frequency Channel Number. Reference: 3GPP TS 38.101-1 section 5.4.2.1 NR-ARFCN and channel raster. Reference: 3GPP TS 38.101-2 section 5.4.2.1 NR-ARFCN and channel raster.
An array of frequency band numbers supported by the cellular network the device is connected to, or empty array if not available.
An array of frequency band numbers supported by the cellular network the device is connected to, or empty array if not available.
